options.a: that can be used by one process at a time and is not depleted by that use
options.b: that can be used by more than one process at a time
options.c: that can be shared between various threads

question: Which of the following condition is required for a deadlock to be possible?

options.b: a process may hold allocated resources while awaiting assignment of other resources
options.c: no resource can be forcibly removed from a process holding it

question: A system is in the safe state if ____________

options.a: the system can allocate resources to each process in some order and still avoid a deadlock

question: The circular wait condition can be prevented by ____________

options.a: defining a linear ordering of resource types

question: Which one of the following is the deadlock avoidance algorithm?
What is the drawback of banker’s algorithm?

options.a: in advance processes rarely know how much resource they will need
options.b: the number of processes changes as time progresses

question: For an effective operating system, when to check for deadlock?

options.a: every time a resource request is made
options.c: every time a resource request is made at fixed time intervals

question: A problem encountered in multitasking when a process is perpetually denied necessary resources is called ____________
Which one of the following is a visual ( mathematical ) way to determine the deadlock occurrence?

options.a: there must be a fixed number of resources to allocate
options.b: resource allocation must be done only once
options.c: all deadlocked processes must be aborted
